(20:11 GMT) *Levi Strauss 1Q Rev $1.74B >LEVI

(20:11 GMT) *Levi Strauss Raises FY26 View To Adj EPS $1.42-Adj EPS $1.48 >LEVI

(20:11 GMT) *Levi Strauss 1Q Direct-To-Consumer Rev Up 16% YoY>LEVI
(20:11 GMT) *Levi Strauss 1Q Wholesale Rev Up 12% YoY>LEVI
(20:11 GMT) Levi Strauss Raises FY Guidance as Turnaround Bears Fruit

By Kelly Cloonan

Levi Strauss & Co. bumped up its fiscal-year guidance following double-digit sales growth in its latest quarter as the apparel company sees its turnaround efforts paying off.

The San Francisco company also said Tuesday it has started a search for a successor to Chief Financial and Growth Officer Harmit Singh, who plans to transition to the role of special adviser before retiring.

Levi's now projects revenue growth of 5.5% to 6.5% for the fiscal year ending Nov. 29, compared with prior guidance of 5% to 6%. It now expects adjusted earnings per share of $1.42 to $1.48, compared with $1.40 to $1.46 previously. The forecast includes a 4 cent headwind from a higher tax rate and excludes Levi's Dockers business, which it has sold.

The updated outlook comes after Levi's logged higher profit and 14% revenue growth in its latest quarter, driven by growth across channels, regions and categories.

Levi's attributed the results to its turnaround strategy, which has included efforts to grow its direct-to-consumer business and sell more clothing items beyond the jeans it's known for. As part of the shift, the company has been expanding its offerings of tops, skirts and dresses and cutting down on its go-to-market timeline to chase trends.

Levi's said those efforts are allowing it to capture a larger market and grow at a faster and more consistent pace.

"Our strategic transformation is translating into higher returns and more profitable growth, enabling us to convert more of our strong revenue growth into bottom-line profit," Singh said, noting the company has also seen positive trends quarter to date.

Singh has led Levi's finances for more than a decade, and took on the added role of chief growth officer in 2023. He has also overseen the company's transformation efforts.

For the fiscal fourth quarter, Levi's posted a profit of $175.8 million, or 45 cents a share, compared with $135 million, or 34 cents a share, a year earlier.

Adjusted earnings per share were 42 cents, compared with estimates of 37 cents a share according to analysts polled by FactSet.

Sales came in at $1.74 billion, compared with analyst estimates of $1.65 billion. Organic sales, a figure that strips out acquisition, divestiture and currency effects, increased 9%.

Direct-to-consumer sales rose 16%, while wholesale sales gained 12%.

Write to Kelly Cloonan at kelly.cloonan@wsj.com

data.title: Formerra Announces Global Price Increase
data.description:
CLEVELAND, Ohio, Apr 8, 2026 - (ACN Newswire) - Formerra, a leading distributor of performance materials, today announced a global price increase of $0.02 / lb on all products. The increase addresses higher operating costs related to rising material costs, exacerbated by the conflict in the Middle East. The adjustment goes into effect on April 15, 2026, or as contracts otherwise allow. Formerra remains committed to maintaining ample inventory levels and the service excellence our valued customers deserve.

Customers should contact their Formerra representative for additional information.

About Formerra

Formerra is a preeminent distributor of engineered materials, connecting the world's leading polymer producers with thousands of OEMs and brand owners across healthcare, consumer, industrial, and mobility markets. Powered by technical and commercial expertise, it brings a distinctive combination of portfolio depth, supply chain strength, industry knowledge, service, leading e-commerce capabilities, and ingenuity. The experienced Formerra team helps customers across multiple industries to design, select, process, and develop products in new and better ways - driving improved performance, productivity, reliability, and sustainability. To learn more, visit www.formerra.com.

Media Contact

Jackie Morris

Formerra

Marketing Communications Manager

jackie.morris@formerra.com

+1 630-972-3144

SOURCE: Formerra

Source: Formerra

Copyright 2026 ACN Newswire . All rights reserved.

data.title: FGI Industries Ltd expected to post a loss of 38 cents a share - Earnings Preview
data.description:
• FGI Industries Ltd is expected to show a fall in quarterly revenue when it reports results on April 9 for the period ending December 31 2025

  • The East Hanover New Jersey-based company is expected to report a 0.3% decrease in revenue to $35.498 million from $35.59 million a year ago, according to the mean estimate from 2 analysts, based on LSEG data.

  • ​LSEG's mean analyst estimate for FGI Industries Ltd is for a loss of 38 cents per share.

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 2 "strong buy" or "buy," no "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts was unchanged in the last three months. ​

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for FGI Industries Ltd is $8.50, about 121.4% above its last closing price of $3.84

data.title: Key facts: Piper Sandler Boosts Akamai Target to $114; Q1 Call May 7
data.description:
• Piper Sandler raised Akamai (AKAM) price target to $114 from $97, a $17 boost reflecting updated valuation. Traders note revised target and potential upside vs. current market price.1
  • Akamai (AKAM) will host its Q1 2026 earnings call May 7, 2026 at 4:30 PM ET. Live webcast, global dial-in numbers and a two-week replay (Conference ID 8699709) will be available.2

data.title: Key facts: Paramount $81B Bid Backed by Gulf Funds; WBD Launches DUNKMAN
data.description:
• Paramount's $81B bid for Warner Bros. Discovery backed by ~$24B equity commitments from Gulf sovereign funds, including ~$10B from Saudi PIF; LionTree joins syndicate; EU regulators review financing.123
  • Warner Bros. Discovery launched DUNKMAN, a joint venture with Shaquille O’Neal, Authentic Brands Group, TNT Sports and Eli Lilly; WBD disclosed the partnership and venture name publicly.4

data.title: Key facts: Nutanix debuts Agentic AI, NKP Metal; Service Provider Central
data.description:
• Nutanix (NTNX) launched Agentic AI in early access, targeting H2 2026 GA. It offers virtualized AI infrastructure on Nutanix Cloud Platform with GPU-as-a-service, K8s, multitenancy and security.1
  • Nutanix (NTNX) launched NKP Metal: bare‑metal Kubernetes with integrated storage, VM/container parity, automation and lifecycle management. Early access to NKP PRO/ULT; GA planned H2 2026.2
  • Nutanix (NTNX) launches Service Provider Central with Nutanix Cloud Platform to offer multitenant IaaS, centralized governance, Prism access and automated workflows; availability slated for H2 2026.3

data.title: Key facts: Netflix Q4 Revenue $12.05B; Goldman Sees Double‑Digit Growth
data.description:
• Netflix Q4 revenue $12.05B (+17.6% YoY), 0.7% above estimates. Next-quarter EPS guide missed expectations; revenue guide met forecasts. Stock traded near $98.62 after a ~13% post-results rise.1
  • Goldman expects Netflix (NFLX) to achieve sustained double‑digit revenue growth from pricing and ads, rising operating margins over the next three years, and significant shareholder returns planned.2

data.title: Key facts: Meta Ordered to Pay $375M; Trades Under 20x Forward
data.description:
• Meta Platforms ordered to pay $375 million by New Mexico court over alleged failure to protect minors. Ruling raises Meta's legal costs tied to platform safety and liability exposure.1
  • Goldman Sachs highlights Meta Platforms (META) trading under 20x forward earnings; flagged as attractive if geopolitical tensions persist and central banks cut rates—key drivers for traders to watch.2

data.title: Key facts: JPMorgan Says NVR Will Lag Builders; Seaport $5,664 Target
data.description:
• JPMorgan says NVR will lag homebuilders this earnings season, expecting cuts to 2026 orders and gross margins due to higher mortgage rates and weaker consumer demand since March.1
  • Seaport Global set a $5,664 per-share target for NVR, Inc. (NVR). No analyst rating, timing or rationale accompanied the target in the firm’s update.2

data.title: Key facts: ENPH Revises Q1 2026 GAAP; Sells $235M Credits=$218.55M
data.description:
• Enphase Energy (ENPH) filed an 8‑K on April 6, 2026: Q1 2026 GAAP gross margin outlook revised from prior guidance; details disclosed under Item 2.02 in the filing.1
  • On March 31, 2026 Enphase Energy sold $235M of 2025 advanced manufacturing tax credits for $218.55M (93% of face) to a financial institution; payment made in one installment.23

data.title: Key facts: Duolingo Q4 Revenue $282.9M; Q1 Results Due May 4
data.description:
• Duolingo Q4 revenue $282.9M (+35% YoY), 2.5% above estimates; MAUs 133.1M (+14.1% YoY). Company logged fastest revenue growth and biggest full-year guidance boost among peers.1
  • Duolingo (DUOL) will report Q1 2026 results for period ended Mar 31 after U.S. close on May 4, 2026, and will host a live webcast at 5:00 p.m. ET that day.2

data.title: Key facts: Broadcom Secures Google TPU Deal to 2031; Anthropic 3.5–5GW, Shares Up
data.description:
• Broadcom secured multiyear deals through 2031 to build and supply custom TPUs and networking gear for Google AI data centers; Anthropic will access ~3.5–5 GW of TPU capacity from 2027.123
  • Broadcom (AVGO) supplied chips for Anthropic’s compute expansion tied to deals adding ~5 GW. Anthropic said run-rate revenue tops $30B; Broadcom shares jumped in after-hours trading.4
  • Broadcom disclosed in a regulatory filing that it has entered into a deal with Google; the disclosure occurred alongside a roughly 1% pre-market decline in Nvidia shares on the same morning.5
  • Broadcom (AVGO) formed a bullish flag after sliding from its December high, finding support near the 100-day EMA. Key resistance looms; a clear break would signal a technical breakout setup.6

data.title: Key facts: Blackstone 14.9% Rev CAGR, 16% EPS; Closes Hologic Buyout
data.description:
• Blackstone reported a five-year revenue compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 14.9% and cited a long-term annual EPS growth rate of 16%.1
  • Blackstone-led buyout of Hologic closed; deal pays $76 cash plus up to $3 CVR per share tied to 2026–27 Breast Health milestones. Hologic delisted from Nasdaq; Joe Almeida named CEO.2

data.title: Key facts: BlackRock Files Nasdaq‑100 ETF; IBIT $500M Inflow; GS Cuts PT
data.description:
• BlackRock files to expand into Nasdaq‑100 ETFs, aiming to add U.S. Nasdaq‑100 offerings to its global lineup and compete with longtime leader QQQ.1
  • BlackRock's IBIT ETF recorded nearly $500 million of inflows into Bitcoin spot ETFs on Monday, marking the first substantial inflows after five consecutive weeks of outflows, according to the report.2
  • Goldman Sachs kept BlackRock (BLK) at a Buy rating and cut its price target for the stock from $1,341 to $1,181 per share, as reported at 18:18 GMT.3

data.title: Key facts: Apple S&P Earnings Leader; Foldable Setbacks, China Patent
data.description:
• Apple (AAPL) is forecast to make about 30% of IT sector earnings for the June quarter and roughly 47% of total S&P 500 earnings, marking it as a dominant earnings contributor.1
  • Apple (AAPL) faces engineering and test setbacks on its first foldable iPhone, slowing component production and risking mass-production and shipment delays from H2 2026 into 2027; no company comment.234
  • China’s Supreme Court upheld an AI patent owned by Xiao‑I, which Xiao‑I says Apple’s Siri infringes, raising potential licensing costs or injunction risk for Apple (AAPL) in China.5
  • AAPL fell about 4–4.4% to $247.43 after reports of engineering setbacks testing a foldable iPhone, warning of potential months‑long shipment delays and supplier production issues.6

data.title: The Bond Market Trusts the Fed's Long Game on Inflation — Barrons.com
data.description:
Karishma Vanjani

An unusually large disconnect has appeared in the bond market, showing just how unsettled investors are about inflation in the near term.

Market expectations for inflation over the next two years have ramped up alongside higher oil prices and supply chain disruptions from the Iran war. But inflation expectations for a decade from now remain well-anchored. This dynamic suggests investors think the war won't have a lasting effect on prices — and that they trust the Federal Reserve to get inflation under control eventually.

That view is captured in the difference between the two-year and 10-year breakeven inflation rates. (These rates are derived from Treasury securities, and essentially predict where inflation will be two and 10 years from now, respectively).

The two-year breakeven was nearly a full percentage point higher than the 10-year on Tuesday. That gap — 0.9402 percentage points, to be exact — is more than three times the average for the past five years, and is near levels last seen a year ago, when President Donald Trump's "Liberation Day" tariffs fueled an inflation scare.

There's a "great divide between short-term and long-term inflation expectations," wrote Cameron Dawson, NewEdge Wealth CIO in a post.

Before the Covid-19 pandemic, it was the reverse: Investors expected inflation to be higher the further out in time they went, which meant the gap between the two- and 10-year breakevens was negative. But the differential has been positive since the very tail end of 2020, and has spiked significantly since mid-February. In 2022, Russia's invasion of Ukraine triggered a similar jump in the gap between the two- and 10-year breakevens.

"We are experiencing our second inflation scare within a five-year period, something that hasn't happened in the U.S. since the inflation-riddled 1970s," Gina Martin Adams, chief market strategist at HB Wealth.

The two-year inflation breakeven rate was about 3.3006% on Tuesday, compared with 2.3604% for the 10-year. The Fed's target inflation rate is 2%.

The market is pricing in "U.S. inflation approaching 4% in the coming quarters," Padhraic Garvey, who leads ING's research team for the Americas, told Barron's.

Still, the market is keeping faith in the Fed and its interest-rate policy. A rather stable 10-year breakeven inflation rate proves that — despite five years of above-target inflation and attacks on the Fed's independence — investors believe the central bank will keep inflation contained over the long-term. The rate has edged up 0.1035 percentage points since the Iran war broke out, compared with a steeper gain of 0.4837 percentage points gain in the two-year breakeven.

"Arguably if the bond market was truly concerned that a captured Fed would cut rates too much and stoke pernicious and persistent inflation, it is unlikely we would see market-based long-run inflation expectations remain so contained," wrote Dawson.

"We have been joking, 'find someone to believe in you like the bond market believes in the Fed's ability to achieve its inflation target'," she added.

However, the market is uncertain about the central bank's moves in the near term. Higher inflation expectations have evaporated hopes for an interest-rate cut at the central bank's April meeting; 97.4% of investors see Fed keeping rates steady while 2.6% are betting on a hike, the CME FedWatch tool shows.

At the same time, with little clarity on the length of the war, a rate cut is possible down the road. That could be necessary if there's a recession because of higher prices at the pump dragging down consumer spending and business hiring.

In that case, "the Federal Reserve is either cutting rates or preparing to do so," Garvey said.

Write to Karishma Vanjani at karishma.vanjani@dowjones.com.

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Nutriband selects commercial brand name for abuse-deterrent fentanyl patch, targets $80M–$200M peak US sales
data.description:
Nutriband selected a commercial worldwide brand name candidate for its lead abuse-deterrent fentanyl transdermal system and will submit the name and labeling to FDA and other regulators.

Key Highlights:

  • Selected commercial worldwide brand name candidate for lead abuse-deterrent fentanyl patch to be submitted to FDA and international agencies.
  • Submitted name for US trademark registration and international IP protection to secure rights.
  • Partnered with Brand Institute and Drug Safety Institute for naming, identity and regulatory review support.
  • Product uses Nutriband’s AVERSA abuse-deterrent transdermal technology designed to deter abuse and reduce accidental exposure.
  • Company estimates potential peak annual US sales of $80 million to $200 million for the product.

Original SEC Filing: NutriBand Inc. [ NTRB ] - 8-K - Apr. 07, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Retail Investors Have Been Hit Hard by Stock Losses-And Bonds Aren't Helping — WSJ
data.description:
By Heather Gillers

It's not just the stock market that has been walloping private wealth portfolios. For the past three weeks, bond portfolios have been losing money, too.

That's one takeaway from an analysis of client holdings published Tuesday by investment-management software firm Clearwater Analytics. The firm looked at returns through Friday on portfolios of $30 million or more. In their worst recent week, the final week of March, bonds accounted for nearly of a quarter-percentage point of losses, helping drag total returns for the week below a negative 3%.

Fears that inflation resulting from rising oil prices will keep rates higher for longer led bonds to sell off that week-and they didn't come back last week.

"Unfortunately, in an energy shock, there are very few winners," said Clearwater head of research Matthew Vegari.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).
